q: A person spends 75% of his monthly income, and he manages to save Rs.4,827 every month. Find his monthly income (in Rs.).
Find the simple interest on Rs.2,000 at 7% per annum for the period from 2 February 2023 to 17 April 2023.
If the fourth proportional of 25 and 8 is 4, then find the value of P.

Tarun goes from his house to his workplace walking at 3/4 of his usual speed, and as a result he reaches 19 minutes late. If he walks at his usual speed, find the time taken to cover the distance between his house and workplace.
A man buys an item for Rs.16. If he sells each item at Rs.2.8, then find his profit percentage.
The present ages of Gopal and Manoj differ by 2 years. If 18 years ago their ages were in the ratio 21:22, then find the sum of their present ages 10 years hence (after 10 years).
A trader marks his goods 37% above the cost price and gives a discount of 16% on the marked price. Find his profit or loss percentage.

The length of one of the two parallel sides of a trapezium is 11.9 cm and the perpendicular distance between the two parallel sides is a cm, while the perpendicular distance between the other smaller parallel side and the smaller side is 3.9 cm and 5.1 cm. Find the perimeter of the trapezium.

A 50 m high tower stands on the ground; from the top of the tower the angle of depression of a car is 30 degrees, and from a point exactly above the tower the angle of elevation is 60 degrees. Find the height of that point above the ground.
Which of the following numbers is divisible by both 2 and 5?
A pipe can fill a tank in 6 minutes, while a second pipe can fill the same tank in 15 minutes. If both pipes are opened together, find in how many minutes the tank will be filled.
A trader buys a washing machine for Rs.7660. If he gives a discount of 12% on its marked price, even then he makes a profit of 10%. Find the marked price of the machine.
The ratio of the monthly incomes of two friends Rakesh and Shyam is 5:6 and their monthly expenditures are in the ratio 1:3. If each of them saves Rs.39,000, then find the monthly income of Rakesh.

Two trains, whose lengths are 248 m and 142 m, are moving in opposite directions on parallel tracks. The first train is running at 34 km/h and the other at 74 km/h. In how much time will they cross each other?

A tank has a hole through which the tank empties. A tap can fill the tank in 7 minutes, and the hole can empty the full tank in 5 minutes. If the tank is empty and both the tap and the hole are open, find in how much time the tank will fill completely.
The sum of two numbers is 32. If one number is 5 more than twice the other number, then find the smaller number.
Three numbers are in the ratio 4/8 : 11/16 : 18/21. The difference between the largest and the smallest number is 40. Find the largest number.
If x + 1/x = 17, then what is the value of (x^2 + 1/x^2)?
The mean of a group of numbers is 53.1 and the median is 14.1. Using an empirical formula, calculate the mode of the numbers.
Simplify the given expression: 189 + 9 + 117 ÷ 13 × 5 − 12 of 4 + 9 × 7
If the total surface area of a cylindrical vessel of height 6 cm is 264 cm^2, then find its volume. (Use π = 22/7)

The price of fuel decreased by 45%, 55%, and 50% respectively in three consecutive months, but in the fourth month it increased by 40%. If the price of fuel had to be brought back to the original (i.e. of two months ago) value, by what percentage must the price be increased?
Find the LCM of the numbers 780, 2080, and 2600.

Select the set in which the numbers are related in the same way as are the numbers of the following sets. (Note: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into their constituent digits. For example, 13 can be operated on as 13; operations such as adding/subtracting/multiplying etc. to 13 can be performed, but 13 should not be broken down into 1 and 3, and then operations performed on 1 and 3.) (424, 212, 212) (508, 302, 206)

Six persons L, M, N, P, Q and R are sitting around a round table facing towards the centre. P is sitting second to the right of N. M is to the immediate right of P. R and M are sitting opposite to each other. L is to the immediate right of R. Who is sitting nearest to L?

Two statements are given followed by two conclusions numbered I and II. Assuming the statements to be true, even if they seem to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the conclusions logically follow(s) from the statements. Statements: All chairs are tables. All tables are benches. Conclusions: (I) Some benches are chairs. (II) Some benches are tables.

From the following number series, all the even numbers have been removed; after removing them, which number would be left in the middle of the series? (Note: all numbers are only one-digit numbers.) 7 4 1 2 4 9 8 5 3 6 1 5 5 4 6 7 4 8 3 9 6 5 2 3 9 1 4 2
On the basis of a particular logic, FTBJ is related to OCKS. In the same way, according to that logic, QEMU is related to which of the following sets of letters?

On the basis of a particular logic, which of the following letter-clusters can be formed in the same way the given letter-cluster is formed? (Note: Operations should be performed on the letter-cluster, without changing the position of the letters, and the number of letters in them should not be changed based on their positions.)
Which of the following letter-clusters should come in place of the question mark (?) in order to complete the given series logically? ZSL 18, VOH 29, RKD 40, NGZ 51, JCV 62, ?
If 'A' means '+', 'B' means '−', 'C' means '×' and 'D' means '÷', then what will be the value of the following expression? (80 A 8) B 5 C 14 B 2 D 11 B 3 C 72 A 4 = ?
Select the option that follows the same pattern as the two given groups of letters. Both the groups follow the same pattern. HQL : ISO, DGS : EIV

Two statements are given followed by two conclusions numbered I and II. Assuming the statements to be true, even if they seem to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the conclusions logically follow(s) from the statements. Statements: All bags are aeroplanes. All aeroplanes are cars. Conclusions: (I) Some cars are bags. (II) Some cars are aeroplanes.

Six persons A, B, C, D, E and F are sitting in a row, all facing the same direction. E is sitting between A and C. A is sitting to the immediate left of B. C is sitting to the immediate left of D. F is sitting to the immediate right of B. D is exactly in the middle of C and B. How many persons are sitting between A and D?
What will come in place of the question mark (?) in the given series? 103 119 135 151 167 ?

If '+' and '−' are interchanged with each other and '×' and '÷' are interchanged with each other, then what will come in place of the question mark (?) in the following expression? 44 + 6 + 195 × 3 − 473 = ?

On the basis of a particular logic, which of the following letter-clusters can be formed in the same way the given three letter-clusters are formed? (Note: The letter-clusters are formed and the number of letters in them is not changed based on their positions.)

Six persons A, B, C, D, E and F are sitting in a row, all facing the same direction. G is sitting at the left end. A is sitting between G and D. C is sitting between A and B. C is not the immediate neighbour of A. Who is sitting at the right end?
In a certain code language, 'let you know' is written as 'nj rc bn', 'know the people' is written as 'kn kp rc'. How will 'know' be written in that language? (All codes are two-letter codes.)
On the basis of a particular logic, what should come in place of the question mark (?) in the given series? DRT UIK LZB CQS ?

Point A starts walking and moves 46 km towards the east. Then he turns right and moves 54 km. Then he turns left and moves 56 km. Then again he turns right and moves 63 km. Then again he turns right and moves 10 km. Finally he turns right and moves 30 km. In which direction should point A travel to return to his starting point? (Note: All turns are only 90-degree turns.)
Select the option that follows the same pattern as the two given groups of letters. Both the groups follow the same pattern. ME-KC-IL, PH-NF-LO
What will come in place of the question mark (?) in the given series? 29 35 42 50 59 69 ?

All 40 students of a class are standing in a row facing the north direction. Rohit is at the 15th position from the right end, while Kapil is at the 22nd position from the left end. How many students are standing between Rohit and Kapil?
